title: Liveblog  Article in live   French municipal elections: Socialist Emmanuel Grégoire leads Paris mayor race
subhead: Voters headed back to the polls in Paris, Lyon, Marseille and cities across France to elect their municipal councils and mayors for the next six years. Follow live coverage of the results here.

title: Italy's Meloni seeks new momentum with a referendum on judiciary
subhead: Italian voters will head to the polls Sunday and Monday to decide on a reform of the judiciary. The vote has all the trappings of a plebiscite for the Italian prime minister. The campaign has been marked by intense polarization and a heavy political climate.

title: Dnipro, the 'heart of Ukraine' where residents, refugees and military live side by side
subhead: This industrial city, considered a stronghold of the Jewish community, embodies the country's history and a certain idea of resistance to the advances of the Russian army, four years after the start of the war.

title: Abou Sangare, from film awards to construction sites and back
subhead: The former undocumented immigrant, crowned most promising male newcomer at the 2025 César awards for his role in 'Souleymane's Story,' was rumored to have been abandoned by the film industry. But Sangare continues to audition and refuses to be cast as a 'victim.'

title: Charif Majdalani: Lebanon is 'truly being dragged against its will into new calamities'
subhead: 'Writers on the War in the Middle East' 2/4. Le Monde asked writers Taleb Alrefai, Charif Majdalani and Lila Azam Zanganeh for their perspectives on the war.
byline: Charif Majdalani,  Lila Azam Zanganeh and  Taleb Alrefai

subhead: The daily interception of thousands of missiles and drones launched by Tehran over the past three weeks has put severe strain on already limited reserves. The situation is particularly critical for France, which is bound by defense agreements with several Middle Eastern countries.

title: Despite exhaustion and fear of Iranian strikes, Israelis continue to support warring government
subhead: Nearly 80% of the population continues to support Benjamin Netanyahu's government after three weeks of conflict with Iran, fueled by the authorities' repeated announcements of victory.

title: French bitcoin evangelical Eric Larchevêque wants his right-wing voice to be part of public debate
subhead: Known to the public as a judge on a television show, the French bitcoin pioneer wants to win over the masses to the cryptocurrencies that made him wealthy. With the 2027 presidential election approaching, this libertarian has embraced increasingly radical ideas.
